class.name: C3Http.Session
class.brief_description: Holds a pool of idle HTTP connections for reuse across calls, reducing the TCP and TLS handshake cost for repeated requests to the same host.
class.description: Create one [C3Http.Session] per logical group of requests and set it on [member C3Http.Options.session]. A [C3Http.Session] is a [RefCounted] and is freed automatically when no [C3Http.Options] objects reference it. One-off callers that leave [member C3Http.Options.session] as [code]null[/code] pay zero cost — a fresh connection is opened each time, as in previous versions.

method.name: close
method.description: Closes all pooled connections and empties the pool. Optional — connections are also freed when the [C3Http.Session] goes out of scope.

method.name: prune
method.description: Evicts all idle connections whose age exceeds [member C3Http.Session.idle_timeout]. Useful after a network change to force fresh connections on the next call.

method.name: checkout
method.description: Returns a connected, non-expired client for [param key], or [code]null[/code] if none is available. Stale or disconnected entries encountered during the search are discarded.

method.name: checkin
method.description: Returns [param client] to the pool under [param key]. If the pool is at [member C3Http.Session.max_connections_per_host] capacity, the oldest idle entry is closed and evicted.

members.member: Maximum number of idle connections kept per unique [code](host, port, scheme, TLS, proxy)[/code] key. Extra connections beyond this limit are closed immediately on checkin.

Seconds an idle connection may sit in the pool before being discarded on the next checkout attempt. Keep this shorter than the server's keep-alive timeout (nginx defaults to 75 s, so 60 s is a safe choice). Set to [code]0.0[/code] to disable time-based eviction.
